hell: noun. A place or situation of great suffering in life.

help: noun. Action given to provide assistance; aid.
hoe: noun. Any of various tools for scraping, scratching, digging, or stirring soil or other materials.
hole: noun. A hollow place or cavity; an excavation; a pit; a dent; a depression; a fissure.

hope: verb. To want (something) to happen, with a sense of expectation that it might [with that (+ clause); or with clause; or with so or not].
loo: noun. A lavatory: a room used for urination and defecation.
loop: noun. A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.
loophole: noun. A slit in a castle wall; today, any similar window for shooting a ranged weapon or letting in light.
lop: verb. To cut off as the top or extreme part of anything, especially to prune a small limb off a shrub or tree, or sometimes to behead
lope: verb. To travel an easy pace with long strides.
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
ole: interjection. An interjection used to stir up excitement.

ope: interjection. An exclamation of surprise; oops.

poh: interjection. Indicating that something is trivial and not worth consideration.

poll: noun. A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.

pooh: interjection. Expressing dismissal, contempt, impatience, etc.

hool: noun. Clipping of hooligan (“a young person who causes trouble or violence, typically as a member of a gang or other group”).

olpe: noun. Originally, a leather flask or vessel for oils or liquids; afterward, an earthenware vase or pitcher without a spout.
oop: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.
